category.name: zoo.collectables.forgottenZoo
category.item:
Collect tools and exchange them for expansions for your hidden zoo.

You get tools in safari and on the wheel of fortune. Then go to the shop and exchange them.

So far you own this many tools:
Want to expand your hidden zoo for %tools% tools and %zd% zoodollars?

category.name: zoo.collectables.easterEvent
category.item: From April 21st to April 27th, you'll experience a flood of Easter eggs! Collect them and exchange them for Easter enclosure decorations, Easter bunnies, and enclosures. Your current Easter egg status:

category.name: zoo.collectables.meadow
category.item: Many grasses, flowers and succulent clover grow in a beautiful, colorful meadow.
In spring, the meadows turn into a sea of ​​wildflowers. And your animals love them!
A transitional meadow is created by clearing trees. Once the forest is gone, small grass shoots will first grow there.
The meadows are also often home to elves, who emerge from their hiding places at night. These shy but friendly creatures often share their homes with hares.
Mushrooms are tasty mushrooms and grow, for example, in North America.
If no one takes care of a "transitional meadow" for a long time, a wild forest will soon grow on it again.

category.name: zoo.collectables.ice
category.item: Not only do penguins and polar bears like cold climates, but the Arctic fox also enjoys ice and snow!
Few people know this, but penguins also like popsicles and sometimes steal them from tourists.
Ice cubes are the perfect way to cool down a hot drink. They provide refreshment on a hot summer day.
Ice skating is an amazing sport. Historians believe that it was invented about 4,000 years ago. The Finns probably contributed to the invention.
Igloo means house to the Eskimos, who are the original inhabitants of North America.
The sparkling icicles form in freezing temperatures. Of course, the warmth of the sun melts them again.

category.name: zoo.collectables.bamboo
category.item: Bamboo is the only food for pandas and grows mainly in Asia.
Bamboo is a collective name for several genera of woody grasses.
Hang a pretty lantern in your bamboo garden and brighten the night with a soft light.
The gong is a nice decoration for your bamboo garden. And that exotic sound... seems to be made to start meditation.
An Asian-style statue will add an atmosphere of calm and peace to the bamboo garden.
People have been making wings from bamboo for a long time. The lightweight yet strong material allows them to reach incredible heights.

category.name: zoo.collectables.parrot
category.item: Some parrots are known to be quite talkative. Some of them even speak in complete sentences.
Parrots are very social and popular pets, but they need plenty of space to run around.
Parrots actually have 4 claws. Two are in front and two in back. That way they won't fall off the branch even if they fall asleep.
Parrots are actually pirates who were transformed into colorful birds by evil magicians.
The jungles of the Amazon region of Peru are home to many parrots, and the rainforest provides them with an ideal habitat.
Parrots like to eat seeds and nuts, but they will also enjoy fruit. It doesn't matter if it's fresh or dried, the main thing is that it's sweet.

category.name: zoo.collectables.rabbit
category.item: Hares live in a variety of areas - from deserts to forests to cold polar regions.
Hares have been around for centuries. Fossils show that hare-like creatures lived in China 60 million years ago.
Rabbit's feet are a symbol of good luck in many cultures.
Once a year, the Easter Bunny arrives and hides chocolate and colored eggs.
European wild hares originally lived in Portugal, Spain and northwest Africa, but they have also reached Australia.
Hares are vegetarians. They prefer to eat grass, herbs, lettuce, and colorful flowers.

category.name: zoo.collectables.penguin
category.item: Penguins are seabirds even though they can't fly.
Penguins are very social and competitive animals that often enjoy dancing underwater.
A penguin's walk on land looks clumsy, but in the water, the penguin is an agile swimmer and diver.
Some people also call a tuxedo a penguin tailcoat because it strikingly resembles the appearance of a penguin.
The Subpolar Islands are home to one of the largest and heaviest penguins - the emperor penguin.
Penguins are absolute gourmets. They prefer fish, but they also enjoy sea creatures.

category.name: zoo.collectables.giantTurtle
category.item: Turtles are reptiles. They most often live near or in water. The largest turtles are water turtles.
Baby giant tortoises communicate with their siblings while still in the egg. They simply bump into each other with their egg shells.
The feet of a water turtle have webbed feet between the toes, while those of land turtles have feet that are more like stumps.
Elephant tortoises can live to be over 100 years old, but they never grow a gray beard.
Elephant tortoises come from the Galapagos Islands, which belong to Ecuador in South America.
Most turtles are omnivores, but some species specialize in only one type of food, such as jellyfish.

category.name: zoo.collectables.lion
category.item: Lions are the second largest felines in the world after tigers.
White lions are actually completely normal lions, only their fur has been bleached by the sun.
They command respect with their large paws and their claws firmly grip their prey.
Although they inhabit savannas and steppes, they are often referred to as the "kings of the jungle".
Today they live mainly in national parks such as in Tanzania or on the coast of East Africa.
Lions, like all felines, are carnivores. They feed on wildebeest, zebras, and buffalo.

category.name: zoo.collectables.peacock
category.item: Peacocks are impressive exotic birds. They roam freely in many zoos and parks.
Peacock tail feathers are beautiful. In Greek mythology, peacock eyes are a symbol of the guardian Argus, who guarded Zeus's lover Io.
Male peacocks are quite vain. They can spend hours combing their beautiful feathers with their claws. And finally they spread them out in all their glory.
Peacocks can do some pretty cool stunts to arouse the interest of females, and females also like to flirt with them.
In India, the peacock has been the national bird since 1963. Indians value these birds because they hunt poisonous cobras.
Peacocks eat mostly grain and grains, but from time to time they will also eat flowers and the occasional beetle.

category.name: zoo.collectables.panda
category.item: The giant panda is a black and white bear native to China.
Baby pandas are very small indeed. They weigh only 100 to 200 grams and are about 15-17 cm long.
Giant pandas have a crooked thumb. They use it to hold food.
Clever pandas use bamboo sticks to skewer their food, just as people eat with chopsticks in Asia.
The last wild pandas live in China, the largest country in the world. Unfortunately, there are only a few of these giant pandas left.
Pandas prefer to chew bamboo, especially the leaves and young twigs. Because they are large animals, they consume a lot of it.

category.name: zoo.collectables.sonja
category.item: The breeder Soňa knows how to take perfect care of the animals. Only she can cuddle all the animals at once.
Monkeys are cheerful companions. They like to play with, for example, brightly colored wooden elephants.
With her cheerfully spinning top, Soňa can cheer up even the grumpiest rhino.
Cute house cats like to chase toy mice, but lions prefer larger prey.
The bear cubs would just cuddle all the time. Some of them don't want to leave their caregiver at all.
Sonia is a master of cuddling. Hire her and you'll get 12% more BZ for her services!

category.name: zoo.collectables.rewards
category.item: ... These great rewards are waiting for you:
